$comment: Neutral shipped <errno.h> (POSIX) — c14 POSIX/SQLite header authoring behind the c13 per-target struct mechanism. THREE surfaces in one header: (1) the `errno` LVALUE is a function-like-free object MACRO `errno -> (*__errno_location())` (glibc/Linux thread-local errno accessor) — the macros surface (c5) splices the #define so `errno = E...` / `if (errno == E...)` expand BEFORE parse into a deref of the libc accessor (an lvalue: deref of the returned int*). (2) `__errno_location` is the libc symbol it calls (dynamically linked from libc.so.6, the FULL-UNIX-build strategy — like `puts`). (3) the E* error numbers are the constants surface (Linux asm-generic values). SCOPE: exactly the E* set SQLite's os_unix.c references (no over-ship); EDEVERR is macOS-only (SQLite #ifdef-guards it) so absent on linux. availableObjectFormats:[elf] — the Linux run target (WSL); macOS errno uses `__error` + different E* values (a c14-macOS follow-on, via a future per-target constants/macros axis), and a pe `#include <errno.h>` stays fail-loud (POSIX-on-windows is the os_win axis). The E* VALUES are interop-critical (a wrong value mis-maps SQLite's errno checks) — verified against the Linux errno-base.h / errno.h.
